pattern: A divide-and-conquer merge sort splits the array in halves, sorts each recursively, and merges the two sorted runs in linear time, giving guaranteed n-log-n ordering without relying on a library sort.
hints: An array of one element is already sorted, so think about how to combine two already-sorted halves.
Merging two sorted lists is a linear walk that repeatedly takes the smaller front element.
Recursively sort the left and right halves, then merge them by comparing the front of each half and emitting the smaller until both are drained.

edge_inputs.description: duplicates must be preserved in count
reverse sorted is the worst case for naive swaps

stress.description: large random arrays with duplicates and negatives across many lengths
stress.note: wide value range with repeats exercises the merge step thoroughly
